finding: The Cloudflare deployment and security-gate workflows contain six remote GitHub Action references that use mutable major-version tags rather than exact 40-character commit SHAs.
design_implication: Treat action-reference immutability as explicit machine-readable supply-chain evidence. Resolve, review, and pin each intended upstream release before treating the workflow source as reproducible.
privacy_boundary: Static public workflow source only; no secret values, private chat, health material, or identifying personal data were read or emitted.
limits: Exact commit pinning is not yet completed because upstream tag-to-commit resolution and review were not available through the current connector route.
A 40-character SHA provides immutability, not safety or trustworthiness.
No Cloudflare deployment, hostname resolution, workflow success, or credential validity is claimed.
The Vercel commit statuses observed for the workflow commit failed due to provider build-rate limits and are unrelated to Cloudflare deployment proof.
falsification_route: Run the committed audit after replacing every remote action tag with a reviewed exact 40-character commit SHA. The result must report status all_remote_actions_immutable with zero unpinned references; independent review must still evaluate the pinned upstream commits.
